questions.text: Why is Allah described as the Lord of the daybreak in this Surah?
questions.answer: Because Allah brings light after darkness, showing He can remove harm and fear.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches believers should rely mainly on charms and superstitions for protection.
questions.answer: False. It teaches direct refuge in Allah.

questions.text: What does seeking refuge from 'what He created' teach about harm?
questions.answer: Harm can come through created things when misused or when Allah allows a test; protection is with the Creator.

questions.text: What is one practical meaning of seeking refuge from darkness when it settles?
questions.answer: Seek Allah’s protection from dangers, fears, and harms that increase in the night or in hidden situations.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about magic and those who blow on knots?
questions.answer: It recognizes magic as a harm and teaches seeking refuge in Allah from its evil.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that fearing magic means a believer lacks tawhid.
questions.answer: False. Seeking refuge in Allah is tawhid; fear becomes wrong only if it replaces trust in Allah.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about envy (hasad)?
questions.answer: Envy is a real spiritual and social harm; believers should seek Allah’s protection from its effects.

questions.text: How can a believer protect themselves from envy in daily life?
questions.answer: Maintain dhikr, recite the Mu'awwidhatayn, avoid showing off, and trust Allah’s decree.

questions.text: What is one healthy way to respond when you feel envy toward someone?
questions.answer: Make dua for their blessing to remain and ask Allah for halal good for yourself; fight envy with gratitude.

questions.text: How does this Surah strengthen a believer’s emotional resilience?
questions.answer: It provides a clear refuge practice that reduces fear and builds trust in Allah.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches external harm is the only danger to a believer.
questions.answer: False. It focuses on external harms; its pair Surah An-Nas addresses internal whispers too.

questions.text: What does 'Al-Falaq' mean?
questions.answer: The Daybreak / The Dawn.

questions.text: Who are we commanded to seek refuge with?
questions.answer: The Lord of the daybreak (Birabbil-falaq).

questions.text: What evils does this Surah teach us to seek refuge from?
questions.answer: The evil of that which He created (Min sharri ma khalaq).; The evil of darkness when it settles (Ghasiqin idha waqab).; The evil of those who blow on knots (An-naffathati fil-'uqad).; The evil of an envier when he envies (Hasidin idha hasad).

questions.text: Who are the 'Naffathati fil-uqad'?
questions.answer: The blowers upon knots (referring to those who practice magic/sorcery).

questions.text: What are 'Al-Mu'awwidhatayn'?
questions.answer: The 'Two Surahs of Refuge' (Al-Falaq and An-Nas).

questions.text: Why do we seek refuge in the 'Lord of the Daybreak' against darkness?
questions.answer: Just as Allah brings the light of dawn to remove the darkness of night, He can remove the 'darkness' of evil from our lives.

questions.text: What does 'Ghasiq' mean?
questions.answer: Darkness, the night, or the moon.

questions.text: What does 'Waqab' mean?
questions.answer: When it enters, spreads, or settles.

questions.text: How can one protect themselves from the 'evil eye' or envy?
questions.answer: By reciting this Surah and seeking refuge in Allah, and by saying 'MashaAllah'.

questions.text: Is this a Makkan or Madinan Surah?
questions.answer: Most scholars classify it as Makkan.

questions.text: How many verses are in Surah Al-Falaq?
questions.answer: Five verses.

questions.text: What does 'blowing on knots' refer to?
questions.answer: A technique used in magic where strings are knotted and incantations are blown upon them.

questions.text: What is the difference between Al-Falaq and An-Nas?
questions.answer: Al-Falaq focuses on external evils (magic, nature, envy), while An-Nas focuses on internal evils (whispers/waswas).

questions.text: Does Allah create 'evil'?
questions.answer: Allah creates everything; 'evil' is the harm that comes from some creations when they deviate or are misused, and we seek refuge in the Creator from that harm.

questions.text: What is 'Hasad' (Envy)?
questions.answer: Wishing for a blessing to be taken away from someone else.

questions.text: Why is the envier dangerous?
questions.answer: Because their malice can lead to harmful actions or the influence of the evil eye.

questions.text: When is it Sunnah to recite these Surahs?
questions.answer: After every prayer, before sleeping, and in the morning and evening.

questions.text: What is the primary message of Al-Falaq?
questions.answer: Complete reliance on Allah for protection from all forms of physical and hidden harm.
